bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
Stop giving advice!

Ask THESE questions instead and let them figure out what they need. 

What do you think of this approach?
transcripcion: So when someone comes to you, this is how you can help people the best. The first thing that's really important is you ask an open -ended question. You don't assume what's going on. You just say to them, what's on your mind? And then they'll start talking. Don't use emotional energy. Don't use cognitive energy. Just listen. Just listen. Be there. Then after they finish talking, they come to a natural pause. You go, oh, cool. What else? And they keep talking, keep talking. You go, oh, cool. And then you keep going. You go, oh, tell me more. Third question now, you get them to think about everything that they just said. and you go, what's the real problem here? You've used no cognitive capacity at this point, and you make them distill what they think the big problem is. And it's fascinating when you do this with them, they go, oh, you know what? Yeah, yeah, I know what it is, problem B. You go, oh, cool. Number four, you go, this is the best part. You go, how can I help? So you don't even come up with a way you can help. They have to think of it. Now, the beautiful thing about this model is, you're teaching people to think for themselves.
script: Entonces, cuando alguien viene a ti, así es como puedes ayudar a las personas de la mejor manera. Lo primero que es realmente importante es que hagas una pregunta abierta. No asumas qué está pasando. Solo diles, ¿qué te preocupa? Y entonces empezarán a hablar. No uses energía emocional. No uses energía cognitiva. Solo escucha. Solo escucha. Está ahí. Luego después de que terminen de hablar, llegan a una pausa natural. Tú dices, oh, genial. ¿Qué más? Y siguen hablando, siguen hablando. Tú dices, oh, genial. Y entonces sigues. Dices, oh, cuéntame más. Tercera pregunta ahora, los haces pensar sobre todo lo que acaban de decir. Y dices, ¿cuál es el verdadero problema aquí? No has usado capacidad cognitiva en este punto, y los haces destilir lo que piensan que es el gran problema. Y es fascinante cuando haces esto con ellos, dicen, oh, ¿sabes qué? Sí, sí, sé qué es, problema B. Tú dices, oh, genial. Número cuatro, dices, esta es la mejor parte. Dices, ¿cómo puedo ayudarte? Entonces ni siquiera se te ocurre una forma en que puedas ayudar. Ellos tienen que pensarla. Ahora, lo hermoso de este modelo es que estás enseñándole a la gente a pensar por sí misma.

bio:
I just talk. 
CLE.
descripcion: #emotionallyunavailaible #emotionalmaturiy #avoidant #communication #relationships
transcripcion: Something I've noticed when you bring a problem up to an emotionally immature person is they can't actually hear you. And I don't mean in the literal sense. I mean they can't hear you because hearing you would mean that they have to sit with that discomfort, to sit with the guilt, the shame that maybe they got something wrong. Because ultimately emotionally immature people experience it is an attack on them rather than feedback of what actually transpired. So instead of listening to understand, they become defensive. They explain, they justify, they minimize, they turn the conversation back on you. And I don't think it's inherently them trying to hurt you. Ultimately, it's a coping mechanism. They're trying to protect themselves from the painful feelings that will get brought up with this rather than try and understand yours. This is also why explaining your pain over and over and over goes unanswered, ignored, or it seems like they don't hear you because ultimately empathy requires self awareness. So that person can only acknowledge in you what they are able to acknowledge in themselves. And this is something that was hard for me to grasp, especially growing up because I always thought conflict was a communication problem. It's not always a communication problem. Sometimes it's a capacity problem. And no amount of explaining can make somebody emotionally available two feelings that they've spent a lifetime trying to avoid. This has been a tough learning curve for me because I always Thought communication was the dual and I'll be all and I'm learning that it's not, it doesn't matter if you know how to communicate. If you have somebody that avoids, if you have somebody that is an avoidant or is dismissive because they avoid their own feelings and they don't want to face themselves, it doesn't matter how well you communicate because it's not going to change the outcome. They are going to do what they do and they're going to be emotionally unavailable and they're going to avoid because that's how they cope and that's how they spent their entire life. Because ultimately, even though they come across self aware, they come across empathetic, they're none of those things. They're exactly who they show you in the times that communication fails and that's an avoidant person.
script: Algo que he notado cuando le planteas un problema a una persona emocionalmente inmadura es que no puede realmente escucharte. Y no me refiero en el sentido literal. Quiero decir que no puede escucharte porque escucharte significaría que tiene que estar con esa incomodidad, estar con la culpa, la vergüenza de que quizás hayan hecho algo mal. Porque en última instancia las personas emocionalmente inmaduras lo experimentan como un ataque hacia ellas en lugar de retroalimentación de lo que realmente sucedió. Entonces en lugar de escuchar para entender, se vuelven defensivas. Explican, justifican, minimizan, devuelven la conversación hacia ti. Y no creo que inherentemente estén tratando de lastimarte. En última instancia, es un mecanismo de afrontamiento. Están intentando protegerse a sí mismas de los sentimientos dolorosos que se suscitarán con esto en lugar de tratar de entender los tuyos. Esto es también por qué explicar tu dolor una y otra y otra vez queda sin respuesta, ignorado, o parece que no te escuchan porque en última instancia la empatía requiere autoconciencia. Entonces esa persona solo puede reconocer en ti lo que es capaz de reconocer en sí misma. Y esto es algo que fue difícil para mí comprender, especialmente creciendo porque siempre pensé que el conflicto era un problema de comunicación. No siempre es un problema de comunicación. A veces es un problema de capacidad. Y ninguna cantidad de explicaciones puede hacer que alguien esté emocionalmente disponible para sentimientos que ha pasado una vida tratando de evitar. Esto ha sido una curva de aprendizaje difícil para mí porque siempre pensé que la comunicación era la solución y lo es todo y estoy aprendiendo que no lo es, no importa si sabes cómo comunicarte. Si tienes a alguien que evita, si tienes a alguien que es evasivo o es despectivo porque evita sus propios sentimientos y no quiere enfrentarse a sí mismo, no importa cuán bien te comuniques porque no va a cambiar el resultado. Van a hacer lo que hacen y van a estar emocionalmente indisponibles y van a evitar porque así es como afrontan y así es como han pasado toda su vida. Porque en última instancia, aunque parezcan conscientes de sí mismos, aunque parezcan empáticos, no son ninguna de esas cosas. Son exactamente quiénes te muestran en los momentos en que la comunicación falla y esa es una persona evasiva.

descripcion: Please communicate your feelings so you're not unhappy #relationship #communication #blahblahblah
transcripcion: It is not your partner's job to figure out how you feel. It is your job to tell them. And whether or not you can do that says a lot about you. Because why are we running social experiments on our partners? You get upset, you say nothing, you drop hints instead. You change your energy, you become distant, and then you do all these things and get mad when they don't notice. But how are they supposed to? No one can read minds. And if you haven't noticed, this is where people start learning horrible habits. every time you expect someone to guess how you feel about something, you are reinforcing the idea that they should read your mind. But it doesn't work that way. And the scary part about all this is that the longer you do this, the more resentment those for you, because you are now upset about a problem that you never even communicated. And that is not fair to you or to them. Communication is not. If they loved me, they'd know. Communication is. I need this, and I'm going to tell you. And yes, I agree it's uncomfortable. But being honest means you have to be vulnerable. You have to risk rejection, risk being misunderstood, risk hearing an answer you don't like. But that is the price of intimacy. And you cannot pick and choose the real meaning of love. So you have to choose it. Okay, so you got this far. Now I'm gonna tell you how you actually fix this pattern. You need to stop reporting behavior to them and start reporting feelings. Instead of saying, you never spend time with me, you need to start saying, I don't feel connected with you. Instead of, you don't really care about me, you need to start saying, I think I need more reassurance. Because people always respond better to honesty than accusations. Don't throw things out there and say, you did this, you did that, but you're not even talking about how you actually feel. And if you keep expecting people to read your mind, you will spend the rest of your life feeling unseen when you never even gave anyone a chance to see you. So please, stop expecting them to figure you out. They can't. It's not their job. Your job is to express how you feel. I'm cam. I'll see you in the next one.
script: No es trabajo de tu pareja averiguar cómo te sientes. Es tu trabajo decirle. Y si puedes hacerlo o no dice mucho sobre ti. Porque, ¿por qué estamos haciendo experimentos sociales con nuestras parejas? Te molestas, no dices nada, das pistas en su lugar. Cambias tu energía, te vuelves distante, y luego haces todas estas cosas y te enojas cuando no lo notan. Pero, ¿cómo se supone que deben hacerlo? Nadie puede leer mentes. Y si no lo has notado, este es el lugar donde las personas comienzan a aprender hábitos horribles. Cada vez que esperas que alguien adivine cómo te sientes sobre algo, estás reforzando la idea de que deben leer tu mente. Pero no funciona así. Y la parte aterradora de todo esto es que cuanto más lo hagas, más resentimiento tendrán hacia ti, porque ahora estás molesto por un problema que nunca ni siquiera comunicaste. Y eso no es justo para ti ni para ellos. Comunicación no es. Si me amaran, lo sabrían. Comunicación es. Necesito esto, y te lo voy a decir. Y sí, estoy de acuerdo en que es incómodo. Pero ser honesto significa que tienes que ser vulnerable. Tienes que arriesgar el rechazo, arriesgar ser malinterpretado, arriesgar escuchar una respuesta que no te gusta. Pero ese es el precio de la intimidad. Y no puedes elegir y escoger el verdadero significado del amor. Así que tienes que elegirlo. Bien, llegaste hasta aquí. Ahora te voy a decir cómo arreglas este patrón. Necesitas dejar de reportar comportamiento y empezar a reportar sentimientos. En lugar de decir, nunca pasas tiempo conmigo, necesitas empezar a decir, no me siento conectado contigo. En lugar de, realmente no te importo, necesitas empezar a decir, creo que necesito más seguridad. Porque las personas siempre responden mejor a la honestidad que a las acusaciones. No lances cosas por ahí y digas, hiciste esto, hiciste aquello, pero ni siquiera estás hablando de cómo realmente te sientes. Y si sigues esperando que las personas lean tu mente, pasarás el resto de tu vida sintiéndote invisible cuando nunca ni siquiera le diste a nadie la oportunidad de verte. Así que, por favor, deja de esperar que te descifren. No pueden. No es su trabajo. Tu trabajo es expresar cómo te sientes. Soy Cam. Te veo en el siguiente.

bio:

descripcion: 5 body language hacks to be more charismatic!
transcripcion: Five body language hacks to help you become more charismatic. This gesture is called computer. If you do this while someone's talking, it shows you're processing, it shows you're listening, and it shows you care. Second, make sure your face matches your words. Don't do this. Oh, I'm so sorry for your loss. Or your loss? Third, imagine yourself playing a game of charades when you're next talking to your friends. This is going to help you increase your gesture vocabulary. Let me show you what I mean. Last night I was playing a game of Xbox and all of a sudden I could hear my dad marching down the hallway as if he was about to kick down my door. He looks me dead in the eyes and he says, are you doing the homework yet, boy? are you doctor yet huh fourth if you struggle with eye contact I'm going to share with you something very powerful this is known as the triangle technique so what you do is every single four to five seconds you shift your eye contact between the left eye to the right eye and then you bring it down to the nose bridge and then you bring it back to the original eye and once you do this successfully it makes everybody who you do feel extremely happy Dan I said happy thank you Permanent marker. Whoops. Keep your hand gestures within what's known as the power sphere. Now you see the power sphere is the area between the belly button and the eyes. Watch what happens if you gesture below the power sphere. Gesturing here shows low levels of charisma, whereas all of a sudden if you move into the power sphere and you start gesturing from this position, this is highly influential and much more engaging. You see, and if you do all of these five tips that I've shared successfully, what you're going to You see, and if you do all of these five tips that I've shared successfully, what you're going to score is a natural five out of five.
script: Cinco trucos de lenguaje corporal para ayudarte a ser más carismático. Este gesto se llama computadora. Si haces esto mientras alguien está hablando, muestra que estás procesando, muestra que estás escuchando, y muestra que te importa. Segundo, asegúrate de que tu cara coincida con tus palabras. No hagas esto. Oh, lo siento mucho por tu pérdida. ¿O tu pérdida? Tercero, imagínate jugando un juego de charadas cuando hables con tus amigos la próxima vez. Esto va a ayudarte a aumentar tu vocabulario de gestos. Déjame mostrarte lo que quiero decir. Anoche estaba jugando un juego de Xbox y de repente pude escuchar a mi papá marchando por el pasillo como si estuviera a punto de derribar mi puerta. Me mira directamente a los ojos y dice, ¿ya estás haciendo la tarea, chico? ¿Ya eres doctor, eh? Cuarto, si luchas con el contacto visual voy a compartir contigo algo muy poderoso, esto se conoce como la técnica del triángulo, así que lo que haces es cada cuatro a cinco segundos cambias el contacto visual entre el ojo izquierdo al ojo derecho y luego lo llevas al puente de la nariz y luego lo llevas de vuelta al ojo original y una vez que haces esto exitosamente, hace que todos los que hables se sientan extremadamente felices. Dan, dije feliz, gracias. Marcador permanente. ¡Ups! Mantén tus gestos de mano dentro de lo que se conoce como la esfera de poder. Ahora ves que la esfera de poder es el área entre el botón del ombligo y los ojos. Mira qué sucede si gesticulas debajo de la esfera de poder. Gesticular aquí muestra bajos niveles de carisma, mientras que de repente si te mueves hacia la esfera de poder y comienzas a gesticular desde esta posición, esto es altamente influyente y mucho más atractivo. Ves, y si haces todos estos cinco consejos que compartí exitosamente, lo que vas a obtener es un natural cinco de cinco.

descripcion:
This was from a call-in episode of the @jeffersonfisherpodcast

Tell them that they deserve that hug. They need to hear it.
transcripcion: I continually ask my husband to hug me if he doesn't, and I would like to know how to get him to want to hug me and not be angry when he doesn't. I'll give you a hug. Everybody deserves a hug. You have to beg him to give you a hug. There's something deeper happening here than just not wanting to hug. just not wanting to hug. I know people who are people people. They want distance. They're not huggers. This doesn't feel like that. Hearing it in your voice doesn't feel like that. Your voice sounds really defeated. This is the question I would ask to have this conversation with your husband. Am I not worth a hug to you? You're going to learn a lot in that answer. You have agency and you get to choose what you're going to do. Is this something that you're wanting to live with? Is this something that you've decided Is this something that you're wanting to live with? Is this something that you've decided, I'm not going to be with anybody who's ashamed to hug me? What I need you to know right now is you are worth that hug. Anybody listening to her now, if this has hit your heartstrings like it's hit mine, tell her that she's worth a hug. You need to know it. And that might be the most important thing you do today, is let her know that she's worth a hug.
script: Continuamente le pido a mi marido que me abrace si no lo hace, y me gustaría saber cómo lograr que quiera abrazarme y no enojarse cuando no lo hace. Te daré un abrazo. Todos merecen un abrazo. Tienes que suplicarle que te dé un abrazo. Hay algo más profundo sucediendo aquí que simplemente no querer abrazar. solo no querer abrazar. Conozco gente que son personas sociables. Quieren distancia. No son abrazadores. Esto no se siente así. Escucharlo en tu voz no se siente así. Tu voz suena realmente derrotada. Esta es la pregunta que haría para tener esta conversación con tu marido. ¿No soy digna de un abrazo para ti? Vas a aprender mucho en esa respuesta. Tienes agencia y puedes elegir qué vas a hacer. ¿Es esto algo con lo que quieres vivir? ¿Es esto algo que has decidido? ¿Es esto algo con lo que quieres vivir? ¿Es esto algo que has decidido?, no voy a estar con nadie que sienta vergüenza de abrazarme. Lo que necesitas saber en este momento es que eres digna de ese abrazo. Cualquiera que la escuche ahora, si esto ha tocado tus sentimientos como ha tocado los míos, dile que es digna de un abrazo. Necesitas saberlo. Y eso podría ser lo más importante que hagas hoy, es hacerle saber que es digna de un abrazo.

bio:

descripcion:
What level do you start understanding me? 🙊

Don’t use jargon to try and sound smarter - you alienate the people you’re talking to.
transcripcion: Recursive deployment of domain -specific nomenclature precipitates a hermeneutical opacity antithetical to transparent ideational transmission. Deploying specialized lexical constructs invariably generates cognitive overhead that undermines comprehension of the foundational concepts. When we introduce unnecessary terminology into communication, we create barriers that obscure the very ideas we're attempting to convey. The more complicated our language, the harder it becomes to understand. convey. The more complicated our language, the harder it becomes to understand what we're actually saying. Simple words work. Complicated ones don't.
script: El despliegue recursivo de nomenclatura específica del dominio precipita una opacidad hermenéutica antitética a la transmisión ideacional transparente. El despliegue de constructos léxicos especializados invariablemente genera sobrecarga cognitiva que socava la comprensión de los conceptos fundamentales. Cuando introducimos terminología innecesaria en la comunicación, creamos barreras que oscurecen las mismas ideas que estamos intentando transmitir. Cuanto más complicado es nuestro lenguaje, más difícil se vuelve entender. transmitir. Cuanto más complicado es nuestro lenguaje, más difícil se vuelve entender qué estamos diciendo realmente. Las palabras simples funcionan. Las complicadas no.

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
I was SO CLOSE! 😬

Comment 'CHAT' if you want my free 2 hour training to stop rambling and speak more clearly.

These are the tips I shared: 
 
1. The voice you have right now isn't your natural voice it's your habitual voice.

2. People form beliefs about you not just based on how you look, but also how you sound. You can buy the way you look, but you can't buy the way you sound. That's earnt.

3. Stop talking at the same speed. Slow down when saying something important, otherwise speed up.

4. Don't rush to answer a question. Pause first - it makes you sound more thoughtful.

ecord a video of yourself speaking for 5 minutes and review it- self awareness is the first step to improving the way you speak

6. End every sentences on a low pitch, it makes you sound more certain.

7. Your posture introduces you before your words do.

8. You become what you practice.

9. Match the expression on your face with the emotion behind your words.

10. Stop trying to be interesting. Learn how to be more interested.

11. Don't report your stories, relive them.

12. Stop replying to people with Yes But - start saying YES AND

13. What you don't change about your communication skills, you are choosing...

14. Your voice is an instrument - you don't use an instrument, you PLAY an instrument - so learn how to play it.

15. Learn how to be vulnerable without bringing the mood down.

Which one's your favorite tip?!
transcripcion: My top communication advice in less than 60 seconds. The voice you have right now isn't your natural voice. It's your habitual voice. People form beliefs about you, not just based on how you look, but also based on how you sound. You can buy the way you look, but you can't buy the way you sound. That's earned. Stop talking at the same speed. Slow down when you're saying something important. Otherwise, speed up. Don't rush to answer a question. Pause. First, it makes you sound more thoughtful. Record a video of yourself speaking for five minutes and then review it. Self -awareness is the first step towards improving the way you speak. End every single sentence on a low pitch. It makes you sound more towards improving the way you speak. End every single sentence on a low pitch. It makes you sound more certain. Your posture introduces you way before your words ever do. You become what you practice. Match the expression on your face with the emotion behind your words. Stop trying to be interesting and learn how to be more interested in others. Don't report your stories for goodness sake. Relive them. Stop replying to people with yes, but, yes, but, and start saying yes, and. What you don't change about your communication skills, you are choosing. Learn how to be vulnerable without bringing the mood down. Your voice is an instrument. without bringing the mood down. Your voice is an instrument. You don't use an instrument. You play with an instrument. So learn how to play it. Yes! Yes!
script: Mi mejor consejo sobre comunicación en menos de 60 segundos. La voz que tienes ahora no es tu voz natural. Es tu voz habitual. Las personas forman creencias sobre ti, no solo basándose en cómo te ves, sino también en cómo suenas. Puedes comprar la forma en que te ves, pero no puedes comprar la forma en que suenas. Eso se gana. Deja de hablar a la misma velocidad. Desacelera cuando estés diciendo algo importante. De lo contrario, acelera. No te apresures a responder una pregunta. Haz una pausa. Primero, te hace sonar más reflexivo. Graba un video de ti mismo hablando durante cinco minutos y luego revísalo. La autoconciencia es el primer paso hacia la mejora de la forma en que hablas. Termina cada oración con un tono bajo. Te hace sonar más seguro. Tu postura te presenta mucho antes de que tus palabras lo hagan. Te conviertes en lo que practicas. Haz coincidir la expresión de tu cara con la emoción detrás de tus palabras. Deja de intentar ser interesante y aprende a estar más interesado en otros. No reportes tus historias por el amor de Dios. Revivilas. Deja de responder a las personas con sí, pero, sí, pero, y comienza a decir sí, y. Lo que no cambies sobre tus habilidades de comunicación, lo estás eligiendo. Aprende a ser vulnerable sin arruinar el ambiente. Tu voz es un instrumento. Sin arruinar el ambiente. Tu voz es un instrumento. No usas un instrumento. Juegas con un instrumento. Así que aprende a tocarlo. ¡Sí! ¡Sí!

descripcion: Have you ever had anybody say this?
transcripcion: Have you ever had somebody say, let me know if you need anything, actually let you know that they needed something? Never. because now you've made it more comfortable on you, and now more of an obligation on them. Of course they don't want to burden you, they don't even want to live. Instead, here's what you do when somebody's grieving. You do the thing. Whatever you thought about doing, go do it. If you wanted to bring them dinner, or said, let me know if you need any food, just go get them food. dinner or said let me know if you need any food just go get them go do the task go run the errand go show up and do their laundry go mow their yard for them go do the thing
script: ¿Alguna vez alguien te dijo, avísame si necesitas algo, y realmente te dejó saber que necesitaba algo? Nunca. Porque ahora has hecho más cómodo para ti, y ahora más una obligación para ellos. Por supuesto que no quieren ser una carga, ni siquiera quieren vivir. En cambio, aquí está lo que haces cuando alguien está de luto. Haces la cosa. Lo que sea que pensaste en hacer, ve y hazlo. Si querías llevarles cena, o dijiste, avísame si necesitas comida, solo ve y consígueles comida. Cena o dijiste avísame si necesitas comida solo ve consigue ve haz la tarea ve corre el mandado ve aparécete y lava su ropa ve corta el pasto por ellos ve haz la cosa

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
The moment someone says “you hurt me” and the other person hears “you’re a bad person”, that’s where every repair attempt dies before it even starts.

Has this ever happened in one of your relationships? 👇

#RelationshipAdvice #CouplesTherapy #AttachmentStyles
transcripcion: You did something that really hurt me, but I'm already dreading telling you because I know what's coming. The defensiveness. The defensiveness. I do care, you know. It's just the second I hear you hurt me, my whole body reads it as, you're a bad person. And the second you defend yourself, my whole body reads it as, your pain isn't real or important or I'm being dramatic. So I'm trying to prove I'm not a monster, and I'm trying to prove I'm not crazy. And neither of us is actually talking to the person in front of us anymore. We're talking to everyone who made it. of us is actually talking to the person in front of us anymore. We're talking to everyone who made us feel this way before. So what do we do? If I can learn to hear your pain without making it mean something about me, and I can learn to share my pain without making it about you, we might actually be able to stay with each other long enough to repair. That's it. That's it.
script: Hiciste algo que realmente me hirió, pero ya estoy temiendo decírtelo porque sé lo que vendrá. La defensividad. La defensividad. Me importas, ¿sabes? Es solo que en el segundo en que escucho que me heriste, todo mi cuerpo lo lee como, eres una mala persona. Y en el segundo en que te defiendes, todo mi cuerpo lo lee como, tu dolor no es real o importante o estoy siendo dramática. Así que estoy intentando probar que no soy un monstruo, e intentando probar que no estoy loca. Y ninguno de nosotros realmente está hablando con la persona frente a nosotros. Estamos hablando con todos los que nos hicieron sentir de esta manera antes. Estamos hablando con todos los que nos hicieron sentir de esta manera antes. ¿Qué hacemos? Si logro aprender a escuchar tu dolor sin hacerlo significar algo sobre mí, y logro aprender a compartir mi dolor sin hacerlo sobre ti, podríamos realmente ser capaces de permanecer juntos lo suficiente para reparar. Eso es. Eso es.

descripcion:
It’s so common for people to only talk about themselves, but that’s never going to build a real relationship.
 
From my pod episode with @jackhneel
transcripcion: You know that friend who always talks about themselves and doesn't talk about anybody else. Like I've seen it where somebody has emailed me and been like, hey, I was on a date and the guy really never asked any questions about me. What should I do? I'm like, don't go on another date with them. If there's no genuine interest in curiosity, then that's not it. You can't train that. There has to be genuine interest because their interest is in something, but if it's just in themselves, that's where it stops.
script: Sabes ese amigo que siempre habla de sí mismo y no habla de nadie más. Como he visto que alguien me ha enviado un correo electrónico y ha sido como, hey, estuve en una cita y el chico realmente nunca me hizo ninguna pregunta sobre mí. ¿Qué debería hacer? Yo soy como, no salgas en otra cita con ellos. Si no hay interés genuino en la curiosidad, entonces eso no es. No puedes entrenar eso. Tiene que haber interés genuino porque su interés está en algo, pero si es solo en sí mismos, ahí es donde se detiene.

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
Start with connection always.

When you've made it to the job interview, most of the time that means you've got the skills.

Now they want to see if they like you as a person. And what better way to show this than through answering the first question of every interview with an origin story that deepens connection with the interviewer?
transcripcion: Instead of answering the question, tell me a little bit about yourself with descriptive words like integrity, intelligent, and the classic, my biggest weakness is that I'm a perfectionist. Instead, tell them a short origin story about yourself. And here's a pro tip. If you can link that story to the reason why you applied for the job, oh, this has got a good connection with the interviewer right from the get -go. And to be honest, it's going to put the rest of the interview on easy mode. Why? Because of connection. Rest of the interview on easy mode. Why? Because of connection.
script: En lugar de responder la pregunta, cuéntame un poco sobre ti con palabras descriptivas como integridad, inteligente, y el clásico, mi mayor debilidad es que soy perfeccionista. En cambio, cuéntales una breve historia de origen sobre ti. Y aquí hay un consejo profesional. Si puedes vincular esa historia con la razón por la que solicitaste el trabajo, oh, esto establece una buena conexión con el entrevistador desde el principio. Y para ser honesto, va a poner el resto de la entrevista en modo fácil. ¿Por qué? Por conexión. El resto de la entrevista en modo fácil. ¿Por qué? Por conexión.

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
The way you speak changes how people perceive you!

People determine your personality through the way you play with your voice.

If you speak in a serious tone all the time, they'll take you to be a serious individual. 

If you always speak with a slightly angry tone, they'll perceive you to be aggressive.

Whereas if you learn how to speak with lots of vocal variety and melody, they'll perceive you to be dynamic, fun person.

What is the way you speak communicating about you?
transcripcion: Your voice is your personality. It determines how people perceive you. No. I'm not reading the damn script. Fuck you. Fuck the script. I don't care how people perceive me.
script: Tu voz es tu personalidad. Determina cómo la gente te percibe. No. No voy a leer el maldito guion. Vete al diablo. Vete al diablo con el guion. No me importa cómo la gente me percibe.

bio:
Lawyer → VP → Founder
Your team isn't the problem. Your leadership system is.
I help 7-fig founders lead without being the bottleneck.
Start Here👇
descripcion:
You got promoted because you were the best at doing the work.

Now you’re being paid to stop doing it.

But nobody taught you how to lead people who do what you used to do so you default to what got you here: telling people exactly what to do and how to do it.

That’s not leadership. That’s just management with a better title.

The shift from expert to executive isn’t about knowing more.

It’s about communicating differently:  less “here’s what to do,” more “here’s where we’re going.”

Direction creates leaders under you.
Tasks create order-takers.

If you’re ready to make that shift, comment EXPERT below.

The Expert to Leader Challenge is coming in April and it only runs a few times a year.

#leader #executive #leadershipcommunication
transcripcion: Leaders don't communicate tasks. They communicate direction. And there's a fundamental difference. When you communicate tasks, you're managing execution. When you communicate direction, you're leading strategy. Now, most experts are stuck in task communication. But at the executive level, your job isn't to tell people what to do. It's to orient them towards where you want them to go and then give them ownership of how they're going to get there. them to go and then give them ownership of how they're going to get there. When you shift from task to direction, you shift from manager to leader.
script: Los líderes no comunican tareas. Comunican dirección. Y hay una diferencia fundamental. Cuando comunicas tareas, estás gestionando la ejecución. Cuando comunicas dirección, estás liderando la estrategia. Ahora, la mayoría de los expertos están atrapados en la comunicación de tareas. Pero a nivel ejecutivo, tu trabajo no es decirle a la gente qué hacer. Es orientarlos hacia dónde quieres que vayan y luego darles propiedad de cómo van a llegar allí. Cuando cambias de tarea a dirección, cambias de gerente a líder.

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Anxious attachment will have you convinced someone is mad at you while they’re just making dinner.

Does this sound familiar? 👇

#RedFlag #ToxicCouple
transcripcion: Can I ask you something? Yeah, of course. Are you mad at me? No, I'm not mad at you. seem mad. I'm not mad, I'm just making dinner. But you're being really quiet, though. Yeah, I'm just chopping vegetables. You chop like that when you're mad. I don't think that's a thing. It's definitely a thing. I promise you I'm not mad. Okay. Are you sure? I'm so happy right now, and I'm really glad to be here with you. You're literally my favorite person. But why are you saying it? with you. You're literally my favorite person. But why are you saying it like that? Like what? Like you're trying to convince me. Because you need convincing right now. Ah, so you are mad. I need you to talk to a therapist. But I am a therapist.
script: ¿Puedo preguntarte algo? Sí, claro. ¿Estás enojado conmigo? No, no estoy enojado contigo. Pareces enojado. No estoy enojado, solo estoy haciendo cena. Pero estás siendo muy silencioso. Sí, solo estoy picando verduras. Picas así cuando estás enojado. No creo que eso sea una cosa. Definitivamente es una cosa. Te prometo que no estoy enojado. Está bien. ¿Estás seguro? Estoy muy feliz ahora mismo, y realmente me alegra estar aquí contigo. Literalmente eres mi persona favorita. Pero ¿por qué lo dices? contigo. Literalmente eres mi persona favorita. Pero ¿por qué lo dices así? ¿Así cómo? Como si estuvieras tratando de convencerme. Porque necesitas convencerte ahora mismo. Ah, así que sí estás enojado. Necesito que hables con un terapeuta. Pero soy terapeuta.

bio:
Communication and mindset tips from a lawyer 🤝
Free Case Evaluation, PDFs, Newsletter, and Podcast👇 ✉️ contact@howtoconvince.me
descripcion:
The strongest response to a backstabber isn’t revenge. It’s adjusting their access while protecting your reputation 🤝

#communicationtips #boundariesmatter #emotionalintelligence #confidencecoach
transcripcion: how to handle a backstabber without looking weak. Step one, don't react. Document. When someone stabs you in the back, your first instinct might be to confront, expose, or explode. Don't. Just stay calm, pay attention, and keep the receipts. Step two, adjust the axis. Once someone shows you they're willing to smile on your face and talk behind your back, the relationship changes. Quietly create space. Don't overshare. Don't confide. Keep it surface level and strategic. They lost Share. Don't confide. Keep it surface level and strategic. They lost access. You didn't lose control. Step three, let your reputation handle it. You don't need to fight every false narrative. People are watching. Let your actions, consistency, and character speak louder than their gossip ever could. Real ones will see the difference and the fake ones, you don't need them anyway. Save this video and follow How to Convince with Lawyer Vince.
script: Cómo manejar a alguien que te traiciona sin parecer débil. Paso uno, no reacciones. Documenta. Cuando alguien te apuñala por la espalda, tu primer instinto podría ser confrontar, exponer o explotar. No lo hagas. Solo mantén la calma, presta atención y guarda los comprobantes. Paso dos, ajusta el eje. Una vez que alguien te muestra que está dispuesto a sonreírte en la cara y hablar a tus espaldas, la relación cambia. Crea espacio tranquilamente. No compartas demasiado. No confíes. Mantenlo a nivel superficial y estratégico. Perdieron acceso. No perdiste el control. Paso tres, deja que tu reputación lo maneje. No necesitas pelear contra cada narrativa falsa. La gente está mirando. Deja que tus acciones, consistencia y carácter hablen más fuerte que sus chismes nunca podrían. Los reales verán la diferencia y los falsos, no los necesitas de todos modos. Guarda este video y sigue Cómo Convencer con el Abogado Vince.

bio:
TX trial lawyer ⚖️
@jeffersonfisherpodcast🎙️
I help people argue less so they can talk more⚡️
Order my book The Next Conversation📙
descripcion:
Waiting until the end of a conversation to give them bad news is always incredibly unkind.
 
Label the conversation first so they know what to expect.
transcripcion: If you're about to have a very sensitive conversation, first thing out of your mouth should be, this is going to be a difficult conversation. You're not going to like what I have to say. This is going to be hard to talk about. I'm not looking forward to this conversation. Or this might come as a shock to you. Any of these little ways to frame, immediately label the conversation, give it a pause, and then give the news. Don't wait until the very end.
script: Si estás a punto de tener una conversación muy delicada, lo primero que debes decir es: va a ser una conversación difícil. No te va a gustar lo que tengo que decir. Va a ser difícil de hablar. No estoy ansioso por esta conversación. O esto podría sorprenderte. Cualquiera de estas pequeñas formas de enmarcar, inmediatamente etiqueta la conversación, dale una pausa, y luego da la noticia. No esperes hasta el final.

bio:
Communication and mindset tips from a lawyer 🤝
Free Case Evaluation, PDFs, Newsletter, and Podcast👇 ✉️ contact@howtoconvince.me
descripcion:
Liars often tell on themselves without realizing it. Pay attention to what they answer, what they avoid, and how hard they work to sound believable 🤝

#communicationtips #bodylanguage #emotionalintelligence #socialskills
transcripcion: Three phrases that expose a liar instantly. One, why would I lie to you? Notice they didn't say, I'm not lying. They answered a question with a question. That's deflection, not denial. Two, I would never do that. Honest people say, I didn't do that. Liars distance themselves from the action instead of the accusation. Three, to be honest with you, if they have to announce their honesty, what were they being before? Save this video and follow How to Convince with Lurevince.
script: Tres frases que exponen a un mentiroso instantáneamente. Una, ¿por qué te mentiría? Fíjate que no dijeron, no estoy mintiendo. Respondieron una pregunta con una pregunta. Eso es deflexión, no negación. Dos, nunca haría eso. Las personas honestas dicen, no hice eso. Los mentirosos se distancian de la acción en lugar de la acusación. Tres, para ser honesto contigo, si tienen que anunciar su honestidad, ¿qué estaban siendo antes? Guarda este video y sigue How to Convince with Lurevince.

descripcion:

What do you think?

And then it gets even deeper because guys are literally doing neck curls, chugging gallons of water, injecting testosterone, hoping for a deeper voice. And this stems from the belief that a deeper voice is attractive. But here's what I've learned over the last 15 years of communication coaching. this is attractive but here's what i've learned over the last 15 years of communication coaching real voice maxing isn't about being deep it's about being dynamic because if i use the deep voice like this and i spoke like this for the entire time you'd probably want to vomit in the back of your throat do you see what i mean that's the equivalent of only playing one key on an entire piano with 88 keys for your entire life notice how in this video sometimes i go high and then sometimes i go low being dynamic is way more important than high and then sometimes i go low being dynamic is way more important than just being deep if you're a guy don't be afraid to play with the high notes have some fun and if you're a gal don't be afraid to go to the lower notes but don't get stuck there voice maxing isn't just about high and it's not just about low it's about playing the whole damn instrument and if you comment the word voice i'll send you my free crash course on how to improve your communication skills fast and that is what
script: He caído en el agujero de conejo más raro esta semana. He empezado a buscar esto que se llama maximización de apariencia. Y luego se pone aún más profundo porque tipos están literalmente haciendo rizos de cuello, bebiendo galones de agua, inyectándose testosterona, esperando una voz más profunda. Y esto proviene de la creencia de que una voz más profunda es atractiva. Pero aquí está lo que he aprendido durante los últimos 15 años de entrenamiento en comunicación. esto es atractivo pero aquí está lo que he aprendido durante los últimos 15 años de entrenamiento en comunicación la verdadera maximización de voz no se trata de ser profundo se trata de ser dinámico porque si uso la voz profunda así y hablara así durante todo el tiempo probablemente querrías vomitar en la parte trasera de tu garganta ¿ves lo que quiero decir? eso es equivalente a tocar solo una tecla en un piano entero de 88 teclas durante toda tu vida nota cómo en este video a veces voy alto y luego a veces voy bajo siendo dinámico es mucho más importante que alto y luego a veces voy bajo siendo dinámico es mucho más importante que solo ser profundo si eres un tipo no tengas miedo de jugar con las notas altas diviértete y si eres una chica no tengas miedo de ir a las notas bajas pero no te quedes atrapada allí la maximización de voz no se trata solo de alto y no se trata solo de bajo se trata de tocar todo el maldito instrumento y si comentas la palabra voz te enviaré mi curso intensivo gratuito sobre cómo mejorar tus habilidades de comunicación rápido y eso es lo que

bio:
Communication and mindset tips from a lawyer 🤝
Free Case Evaluation, PDFs, Newsletter, and Podcast👇 ✉️ contact@howtoconvince.me
descripcion:
HR can only act on what they can document. Stick to facts, create a paper trail, and leave the emotions out of it 🤝

#communicationtips #workplaceconfidence #professionalcommunication #careerstrategy
transcripcion: what to say when you go to HR and what not to say. Step one, document, not complain. Instead of saying, I need to talk about someone who's stressing me out, say this, I want to document a situation so there's a clear record. Step two, be specific. Instead of saying, they're always rude, say, on this specific date, this happened and it affected my ability to work. Step three, stay neutral. Instead of saying, I want you to punish them, say this, I'm sharing this so we I want you to punish them. Say this, I'm sharing this so we can find a productive path forward. Step four, create accountability. Instead of saying, so what now? Say, can you confirm this is being documented and what the next steps are? And here's an advanced move. Instead of saying, just get me posted. Say, please send written confirmation once the follow -up is completed. Save this video and follow How to Convince with Willier Vince.
script: Qué decir cuando vas a Recursos Humanos y qué no decir. Paso uno, documentar, no quejarse. En lugar de decir, necesito hablar sobre alguien que me está estresando, di esto, quiero documentar una situación para que haya un registro claro. Paso dos, sé específico. En lugar de decir, siempre son groseros, di, en esta fecha específica, esto sucedió y afectó mi capacidad para trabajar. Paso tres, mantente neutral. En lugar de decir, quiero que los castigues, di esto, quiero compartir esto para que podamos encontrar un camino productivo hacia adelante. Paso cuatro, crea responsabilidad. En lugar de decir, ¿y ahora qué? Di, ¿puedes confirmar que esto se está documentando y cuáles son los próximos pasos? Y aquí hay un movimiento avanzado. En lugar de decir, solo consígueme trasladado. Di, por favor envía confirmación escrita una vez que se complete el seguimiento. Guarda este video y sigue Cómo Convencer con Willier Vince.

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication 🚀
Free 2 Hour Training 👇
descripcion:
Never answer a question quickly.

When someone asks you something, the worst thing you can do is rush straight into your answer. Because when you rush, you look nervous. When you look nervous, you lose credibility. And when you lose credibility, your answer doesn't land the way it should.

Here's what to do instead: pause. Take a breath. Let the question settle for just a second or two. That pause does three things. First, it gives you time to organise your thoughts. Second, it signals confidence because confident people don't need to fill every silence. Third, it makes the other person feel heard because you're actually considering what they asked.

Most people think pausing makes them look slow or unsure. It's the opposite. The pause makes you look thoughtful, controlled, and present.

Next time someone asks you a question, try this. Pause. Breathe. Then answer. You'll notice the difference immediately, and so will they.
transcripcion: People tend to think that answering a question quickly makes them look good, makes them look smart. When you answer a question really quickly, it doesn't do that. It makes it seem as if you didn't put any thought into the answer. People don't want a rushed answer. They want an answer that adds the most value to their lives and people are willing to wait for that. When you ask me this, if I paused and then now I answer your question, that's a confident pause. That's a pause of me processing what you're saying, carefully considering what I'm going to say next, and then sharing my thoughts.
script: La gente tiende a pensar que responder una pregunta rápidamente los hace verse bien, los hace verse inteligentes. Cuando respondes una pregunta realmente rápido, no hace eso. Hace parecer como si no hubieras puesto ningún pensamiento en la respuesta. La gente no quiere una respuesta apresurada. Quieren una respuesta que agregue el mayor valor a sus vidas y la gente está dispuesta a esperar por eso. Cuando me haces esto, si hago una pausa y entonces ahora respondo tu pregunta, esa es una pausa confiada. Esa es una pausa de mí procesando lo que estás diciendo, considerando cuidadosamente lo que voy a decir a continuación, y entonces compartiendo mis pensamientos.
